description: HP 8673B synthesized signal generator (microwave, 2.0 to 26.0 GHz; 1.95 to 26.5 GHz overrange). Legacy HP-IB (IEEE-488) instrument, NOT SCPI, and has NO identify query. All functions except the LINE switch are HP-IB programmable. Program codes are typically 2-character mnemonics, each normally equivalent to a front-panel keystroke. A Data (program) message is built as [Program Code][Numeric Value][Units Terminator][EOS], or [Program Code][Numeric Value][EOS], or [Program Code][EOS]. End-of-String (EOS) may be Line Feed (LF), semicolon, or bus END (EOI). Units terminators scale the numeric value: frequency in GZ (GHz), MZ (MHz), KZ (kHz) or HZ (Hz); dwell time in MS (milliseconds); power in DB or DM (dBm). Alpha characters may be upper, lower or mixed case; spaces, unnecessary signs, leading zeros and CR are ignored. Default Immediate Execution mode (set by @3); Deferred Execution mode set by @2. Value-selectable parameters may be read back by appending OA (Output Active) to the program code and addressing the instrument to talk. Interface function codes: SH1, AH1, T5, TE0, L3, LE0, SR1.

commands.name: frequency
commands.description: Set CW frequency (preferred code FR; CF or CW also accepted). CW also turns off any active sweep mode. Followed by numeric value and units terminator (GZ/MZ/KZ/HZ). Range 2.0 to 26.0 GHz. Read back the set CW frequency with FROA (returned in Hz); read the current RF output frequency with OK.

commands.name: frequency_increment
commands.description: Set the frequency increment (step) used by UP/DN tuning (preferred code FI; FN or Fl also accepted). Followed by numeric value and units terminator.

commands.name: increment_up
commands.description: Increment CW (or center) frequency by the frequency increment; in MANUAL sweep mode increments by the step size. One step per code received; send multiple codes to step multiple times.

commands.name: increment_down
commands.description: Decrement CW (or center) frequency by the frequency increment; in MANUAL sweep mode decrements by the step size. One step per code received; send multiple codes to step multiple times.

commands.name: tune_knob_off
commands.description: Disable the front-panel TUNE knob (N0 or NO).

commands.name: tune_knob_on
commands.description: Enable the front-panel TUNE knob (active in local mode only).

commands.name: offset_frequency
commands.description: Set frequency offset for downconverter/harmonic-mixing applications (preferred code FT; FO also accepted). Positive offset needs no sign. Entering an offset of zero deactivates offset mode. The offset is not multiplied by the display multiplier.

commands.name: display_multiplier
commands.description: Set the display frequency multiplier (MU or MY) followed by the multiplier number and the XF terminator. All frequencies (except offset) are multiplied by this value for display.

commands.name: rf_output_level
commands.description: Set RF output level directly (preferred code LE; AP or PL also accepted). Followed by numeric value and power units terminator DB or DM (dBm). Settable range approximately -100 to +13 dBm. Read back with LEOA.

commands.name: output_level_range
commands.description: Set output level RANGE (step attenuator), in 10 dB steps from +10 to -90 dB. The 0 to -90 dB ranges directly control a 90 dB step attenuator. Used with the VERNIER to set output level separately from the direct LE code.

commands.name: output_level_vernier
commands.description: Set the VERNIER (ALC) level setting. Continuously variable -10 to +3 dBm (ALC controls -10 to +13 dBm); remote DAC resolution is 0.1 dB. Used together with RANGE (RA) to set output level.

commands.name: range_down
commands.description: Decrease RANGE (output level) by 10 dB.

commands.name: range_up
commands.description: Increase RANGE (output level) by 10 dB.

commands.name: rf_output_off
commands.description: Turn the RF output off (RF0 or RO).

commands.name: rf_output_on
commands.description: Turn the RF output on (RF1 or R1). Turning on the RF output starts an Auto Peak operation; monitor the SOURCE SETTLED status bit for completion.

commands.name: am_off
commands.description: Turn amplitude modulation off (A0/AO or A1).

commands.name: am_30_percent
commands.description: Select the 30% AM-per-volt depth range. Actual AM depth is set by the amplitude of the external modulation source at the AM IN connector.

commands.name: am_100_percent
commands.description: Select the 100% AM-per-volt depth range. Actual AM depth is set by the amplitude of the external modulation source at the AM IN connector.

commands.name: fm_off
commands.description: Turn frequency modulation off (D0/DO or D1).

commands.name: fm_deviation
commands.description: Select the FM peak-deviation range. D2=0.03 MHz, D3=0.1 MHz, D4=0.3 MHz, D5=1 MHz, D6=3 MHz, D7=10 MHz. Actual deviation is set by the amplitude of the external modulating signal at the FM IN connector.

commands.name: pulse_off
commands.description: Turn pulse modulation off (P0/PO or P1).

commands.name: pulse_normal
commands.description: Select pulse normal mode. Pulse modulation is TTL-level compatible at the PULSE IN connector.

commands.name: pulse_complement
commands.description: Select pulse complement mode.

commands.name: sweep_off
commands.description: Turn sweep mode off (W0/WO or W1).

commands.name: auto_sweep
commands.description: Select automatic (repetitive) sweep mode. Steps the RF output from start frequency to stop frequency and repeats.

commands.name: manual_sweep
commands.description: Select manual sweep mode (W3; SM also documented). Limits the tuning range between start and stop frequency; use IF / UP / DN to step.

commands.name: single_sweep_arm_execute
commands.description: Single sweep: arm, or execute if already armed.

commands.name: single_sweep_arm_only
commands.description: Single sweep: arm only (does not execute immediately). Follow with W4 to execute.

commands.name: single_sweep_arm_and_begin
commands.description: Single sweep: arm and begin (perform one sweep immediately).

commands.name: master_sweep
commands.description: Select master sweep mode (master/slave sweep configuration).

commands.name: slave_sweep
commands.description: Select slave sweep mode (master/slave sweep configuration).

commands.name: reset_sweep
commands.description: Reset the sweep.

commands.name: center_frequency
commands.description: Set the sweep center frequency (preferred code CF; FR also accepted). Followed by numeric value and units terminator. Read back with CFOA (returned in Hz).

commands.name: delta_frequency
commands.description: Set the sweep delta frequency (span). Followed by numeric value and units terminator. Read back with DFOA (returned in Hz). Setting delta frequency does not change the CW frequency when sweep is off.

commands.name: start_frequency
commands.description: Set the sweep start frequency. Followed by numeric value and units terminator.

commands.name: stop_frequency
commands.description: Set the sweep stop frequency. Followed by numeric value and units terminator. Setting stop lower than start produces a negative (down) sweep span.

commands.name: sweep_steps_and_size
commands.description: Set the number of sweep steps or the sweep step size, both using code SP (SS interchangeable). With the SS units terminator the value is the number of sweep steps (1 to 9999). With a frequency units terminator (GZ/MZ/KZ/HZ) the value is the sweep step size. Read back step size and number of steps with SPOA, which returns: SP <step size> HZ, SP <number of steps> SS.

parameters.name: value
parameters.range: number of steps 1 to 9999, or step size in frequency units

commands.name: dwell_time
commands.description: Set the sweep dwell time per step. Followed by numeric value and the MS units terminator. Read back with DWOA.

commands.name: increment_frequency_sweep
commands.description: Increment frequency in MANUAL sweep mode only (see manual sweep).

commands.name: markers_off
commands.description: Turn all sweep markers off (M0/MO or X0/XO).

commands.name: marker_activate
commands.description: Activate sweep marker 1-5 (M1-M5, or X1-X5). When followed by a frequency value and units terminator, sets that marker's frequency.

commands.name: marker_frequency
commands.description: Set a sweep marker frequency: marker identifier (M1-M5 or X1-X5) followed by the frequency value and units terminator (GZ/MZ/KZ/HZ).

commands.name: step
commands.description: STEP function code (SF; SP also documented for STEP). Used in step/sweep operations.

commands.name: auto_peak_off
commands.description: Turn AUTO PEAK off.

commands.name: auto_peak_on
commands.description: Turn AUTO PEAK on. K2 enables AUTO PEAK without the extra settling time.

commands.name: meter_level
commands.description: Set the front-panel meter to LVL (output level) mode.

commands.name: meter_am
commands.description: Set the front-panel meter to AM (depth) mode.

commands.name: meter_fm
commands.description: Set the front-panel meter to FM (deviation) mode.

commands.name: instrument_preset
commands.description: Preset the instrument to its register-0 (default) state (IP, or RC0 / RCO). RCBS selects the alternate preset (firmware 2552A, 2634A and above).

commands.name: store
commands.description: Store the current instrument state into a storage register. Followed by the register number (1-9).

commands.name: recall
commands.description: Recall an instrument state from a storage register (RC or RL). Followed by the register number (1-9); RC0 presets the instrument.

commands.name: freq_display_off
commands.description: Turn the FREQUENCY display off.

commands.name: freq_display_on
commands.description: Turn the FREQUENCY display on.

commands.name: message
commands.description: Read the MESSAGE (error/malfunction code). After sending MG and addressing the instrument to talk, it returns a two-digit ASCII code followed by LF and EOI. Reading the message clears it to 00 (No Error).

commands.name: clear_status
commands.description: Clear the status byte and extended status byte.

commands.name: output_status
commands.description: Output the status byte and extended status byte (2 binary bytes with EOI) when addressed to talk.

commands.name: output_request_mask
commands.description: Output the Request (RQS) mask in binary (1 byte with EOI) when addressed to talk.

commands.name: set_request_mask
commands.description: Set the service-request (RQS) mask, in binary (prefix RM, or @1).

commands.name: configure_trigger
commands.description: Configure the instrument's response to a Trigger message. CT is followed by one valid program code; that code is executed when a Trigger (bus GET or TR) message is received. Example: CTW6 performs a single sweep on trigger.

commands.name: execute_trigger
commands.description: Execute the pre-configured trigger response (Data-message equivalent of the bus Group Execute Trigger). Acts only if a response was set via Configure Trigger (CT).

commands.name: output_active_parameter
commands.description: Output Active Parameter suffix. Append OA to a value-selectable program code (e.g. CFOA, FROA, FAOA, FBOA, FSOA, M1OA-M5OA, DWOA, LEOA, VEOA, RAOA) then address to talk to read its current value as [Program Code][Numeric Value][Units Terminator][LF and EOI]. SPOA returns step size and number of steps.

commands.name: output_lock_frequency
commands.description: Output the current RF output (locked) frequency. After sending OK and addressing to talk, returns FR <value> Hz [LF and EOI]. Should not be used while sweeping.

commands.name: output_couple
commands.description: Output Couple. When addressed to talk after OC, returns the current numeric values for [START], [Center Frequency], [DWELL] (start and center in Hz, dwell in ms) followed by LF and EOI.

commands.name: front_panel_learn_mode
commands.description: Front Panel Learn Mode (L1; OL also documented). When addressed to talk, the instrument sends @A followed by a 94-byte binary front-panel configuration string (96 bytes total) with EOI. The same binary string can be sent back (after @A) to restore the state.

commands.name: special_function_learn_mode
commands.description: Special Function Learn Mode (service). When addressed to talk, the instrument sends @9 followed by a 26-byte binary string with EOI. See Service documentation.

commands.name: test_interface
commands.description: Test Interface (service). Followed by 1 byte; when addressed to talk the instrument echoes the byte just received (1 byte with EOI).

commands.name: service_function
commands.description: Service Function code (SV). Service/diagnostic use.

commands.name: immediate_execution_mode
commands.description: Select Immediate Execution mode (default at turn-on). Each ASCII character is processed before the next is accepted; the instrument produces its own EOS after each character.

commands.name: deferred_execution_mode
commands.description: Select Deferred Execution mode. The instrument accepts strings up to 96 characters and executes upon receiving an EOS (LF, semicolon, bus END, or @). Faster transfers for fast controllers.

commands.name: slave_up
commands.description: Slave Up (master/slave sweep coordination).

commands.name: slave_down
commands.description: Slave Down (master/slave sweep coordination).

commands.name: backspace
commands.description: Backspace during numeric entry (firmware 2552A, 2634A and above).
